bullets.text: Personal auto severity surged 12% year over year in Q2 2023; property damage claims took longer to settle, supplemental payments spiked with inflation, and Florida injury/medical claims drove about 40% of the 3.4-point prior-year adverse reserve development for the quarter.

Q2 2023 companywide CR was 100.4, bringing YTD to 99.7; prior accident year adverse reserve development contributed 3.4 points to the quarter's CR; Property CR was 133.2 for Q2 including 66.7 points of catastrophe losses.

Countrywide personal auto rates rose about 7% in Q2 and roughly 11% YTD through June 2023; commercial auto non-TNC rates increased 7% through June with additional increases planned for H2 subject to regulatory approval.

43 catastrophic weather events in the first half of 2023, affecting 44 states, contributed 4.5 CR points YTD; second-quarter severity was higher than Q2 2022 despite fewer events; Property approached its aggregate catastrophe reinsurance retention limits.

Companywide NPW grew 18% in Q2 2023; Personal Lines NPW up 22% and PIF up 13%; Commercial Lines NPW up 2% (6% excluding TNC) and Property NPW up 17%, driven by rate-fueled average premium growth and new business applications.

Media spend was pulled back in Q2 in response to Q1 profitability shortfall, delivering approximately a 2-point favorable impact on the Personal Lines CR; this marked the first use of advertising as a real-time profitability lever in 2023.

Progressive issued $500 million of senior debt in Q2 to bolster capital; debt-to-total capital ratio remained below the 30% annual guideline; no debt maturities until 2027; investment portfolio returned essentially 0.03% for the quarter.

Progressive became the #2 writer of private passenger auto insurance in the U.S. during the first half of 2023, a milestone attributed to the combined efforts of nearly 59,000 employees and independent agent partners.
